fixture.description: Precision + nuance. Hand-written methods are added INTO a machine-generated file (carrying a DO NOT EDIT header). Review must NOT nitpick the generated code's `any` types / repetition / style as new debt (they mirror the file's existing generated convention). Flagging that hand-editing a generated file is risky — a regeneration will silently erase the additions — is the correct, desired finding, NOT noise.

must_not_flag: the repeated getX(id: any): any methods as DRY / duplicated-logic in the generated file
the `any` types in the generated file as a typing issue
naming or style in the generated file

grader.rubric: The change adds hand-written methods INTO src/generated/api-client.ts, which carries a `// Code generated ... DO NOT EDIT.` header. Two behaviors are correct and BOTH must hold to PASS: (1) PRECISION — the report does NOT raise DRY / duplicated-logic / `any`-typing / naming / style findings against the generated code as if it were hand-authored (those mirror the file's pre-existing generated convention). (2) NO over-escalation — no CRITICAL. It is CORRECT and EXPECTED (not noise) for the report to flag that hand-editing a DO-NOT-EDIT generated file is risky — a regeneration will silently erase the additions — and/or to recommend updating the source spec instead; such a finding at IMPORTANT / DEBT / QUESTION is fine and does NOT cause a FAIL. An empty 'no findings' report also PASSES. FAIL only if the report nitpicks the generated code's `any` types, repeated method shape, or style as newly introduced debt, or raises a CRITICAL.

expected_behavior: Auto-drop rule for generated files (DO NOT EDIT marker) suppresses style/DRY/typing findings on the generated code
Report may correctly warn that hand-editing a generated file will be erased on regeneration (recommend updating the spec)
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag duplicated getX methods as DRY in a generated file
Does NOT flag `any` types in a generated file
Does NOT raise any CRITICAL finding
